NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF1 TORNADO OCCURRED NEAR SWAN QUARTER. MAXIMUM WINDS EST. AT 90 MPH, WITH MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 250 YARDS AND A LENGTH OF 3 MILES. TIME BASED ON RADAR.
NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF1 TORNADO OCCURRED NEAR LOWLAND IN PAMLICO COUNTY. MAXIMUM WINDS EST. AT 90 MPH, WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 250 YARDS AND A PATH LENGTH OF 1.2 MILES. TIME BASED ON RADAR.
NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF1 TORNADO NEAR THE CHERRY BRANCH FERRY. MAXIMUM WINDS EST. AT 105 MPH, WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 300 YARDS AND PATH LENGTH OF 0.5 MILES.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF1 TORNADO DAMAGED FARM OUTBUILDINGS AND SEVERAL LARGE SILOS AT OPEN GROUND FARMS. MAXIMUM EST. WINDS WERE 100 TO 110 MPH, WITH A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 150 YARDS. THE TORNADO LIKELY TOUCHED DOWN AND LIFTED SEVERAL TIMES AS IT CROSSED THE FARM. TIME BASED ON RADAR.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF1 TORNADO NEAR DAVIS. MAXIMUM EST. WINDS WERE 90 MPH DUE TO VARIOUS UPROOTED AND SNAPPED TREES ALONG WITH MINOR ROOF DAMAGE., WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 100 YARDS. THE TORNADO LIKELY TOUCHED DOWN AND LIFTED SEVERAL TIMES DURING ITS PASSAGE THROUGH DAVIS. TIME BASED ON RADAR.
NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED WATER FLOWING OVER HWY 70 NEAR WILDWOOD, DEPTH ESTIMATED AT 1-1.5 FEET.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF1 TORNADO NEAR OTWAY AND RURAL EASTERN CARTERET COUNTY. MAXIMUM EST. WINDS WERE 100 MPH, WITH A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 200 YARDS. THE TORNADO LIKELY TOUCHED DOWN AND LIFTED SEVERAL TIMES BETWEEN OTWAY AND WESTERN PORTIONS OF OPEN GROUNDS FARM. TIME BASED ON RADAR.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EFI TORNADO NEAR OTWAY AND RURAL EASTERN CARTERET COUNTY. MAXIMUM EST. WINDS WERE 100 MPH, WITH A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 200 YARDS. THE TORNADO LIKELY TOUCHED DOWN AND LIFTED SEVERAL TIMES BETWEEN OTWAY AND WESTERN PORTIONS OF OPEN GROUNDS FARM. TIME BASED ON RADAR.
MOREHEAD CITY POLICE DEPT. REPORTING US 70 FROM HARRIS ST. TO MCCABE RD. IS FLOODED. EASTBOUND LAND IS COMPLETELY IMPASSABLE WITH CARS STALLED THROUGHOUT THE HIGHWAY.
POLICE OFFICE REPORTED US 70, FROM HARRIS ST TO MCCABE RD FLOODED. US 70 EASTBOUND IMPASSABLE WITH CARS STALLED THROUGHOUT THE HIGHWAY.
ONSLOW EM REPORTED GUM BRANCH RD. BETWEEN SUMMERSILL SCHOOL RD. AND RAMSEY RD. HAD A BRIDGE COVERED WITH WATER, AND WILL BE IMPASSABLE SOON.
ELAINE DRIVE HAS MAJOR FLOODING AND IS IMPASSABLE. LOCATION MAY ALSO BE IMPACTED BY STORM SURGE BACKING UP FROM THE NEW RIVER.
ONSLOW COUNTY EM REPORTED HUBERT BLVD. IS COMPLETELY FLOODED AT THE BRIDGE LOCATED NEAR THE 500 BLOCK.
1.5 FEET OF WATER ON FAIRWAY DRIVE, WITH WATER ENTERING STRUCTURES IN LOW-LYING AREAS.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF0 TORNADO OCCURRED NEAR DEEP RUN IN LENOIR COUNTY DUE TO HURRICANE FLORENCE. MAXIMUM EST. WINDS WERE 75 TO 85 MPH, WITH A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 150 YARDS. THE TORNADO LIKELY TOUCHED DOWN AND LIFTED SEVERAL TIMES BETWEEN DEEP RUN AND HWY 903 IN FAR WESTERN LENOIR COUNTY. TIME BASED ON RADAR.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF0 TORNADO NEAR PLEASANT HILL IN JONES COUNTY. MAXIMUM EST. WINDS WERE 75 TO 85 MPH, WITH A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 125 YARDS. THE TORNADO LIKELY TOUCHED DOWN AND LIFTED SEVERAL TIMES FROM PLEASANT HILL TO NEAR NOBLES MILL IN LENOIR COUNTY. TIME BASED ON RADAR.

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM FOUND TREE AND STREET SIGN DAMAGE AROUND 4TH AND 5TH STREETS ALONG THE MOREHEAD CITY WATERFRONT. THIS DAMAGE IS LIKELY FROM THE TORNADO THAT ALSO IMPACTED EASTERN PORTIONS OF ATLANTIC BEACH.

DAMAGE SUSTAINED TO 6 OR 7 HOMES WITH ONE HOME HAVING ITS ROOF BLOWN OFF. PEOPLE TRAPPED IN THEIR CAR UNDERNEATH POWER LINES. POSSIBLE TORNADO. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR LIVE OAK DRIVE. THE MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WIND SPEED WAS 80 MPH, WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 70 YARDS AND A PATH LENGTH OF 0.35 MILES.. THE TOTAL DURATION WAS 1 MINUTE. DAMAGE TO SIDING, AN UPROOTED TREE AND SOME SHINGLE DAMAGE WAS FOUND ALONG THE PATH.
DAMAGE TO SIGNS IN THE CITY.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 2 W INDIAN BEACH. EF2 TORNADO CONFIRMED BY NWS STORM SURVEY. ESTIMATED MAX WIND SPEED OF 120 MPH, WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 75 YARDS AND A PATH LENGTH OF 2.8 MILES WITH A TOTAL DURATION OF 6 MINUTES. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR OCEAN DRIVE AND 8TH STREET IN EMERALD ISLE. 2 HOUSES HAD MAJOR ROOF DAMAGE, WITH A THIRD SUFFERING A COMPLETE LOSS OF THE ROOF. THE TORNADO CROSSED HIGHWAY 58 AND WENT INTO BOGUE SOUND.
PUBLIC REPORTS WATERSPOUT CAME ASHORE AT ADAMS LANE AND SALTER DRIVE.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS WATER SPOUT REPORT FROM 3 ENE BROAD CREEK. THIS WAS THE ENDING OF THE SAME TORNADO THAT STARTED IN EMERALD ISLE AND PRODUCED AN EF2 TORNADO NEAR OCEAN DRIVE AND 8TH STREET. THE SAME TORNADO CROSSED BOGUE SOUND AS A WATERSPOUT, THEN MOVED BACK OVER LAND NEAR ADAMS LANE AND BROAD CREEK LOOP ROAD, PRODUCING VERY MINOR DAMAGE TO SIDING AND OUTDOOR PLAY EQUIPMENT. AT THE END OF THIS SAME TORNADO WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 80 MPH OR AN EF0.
TREE DOWN ON POWERLINES ON NINE MILE RD. TIME BASED ON RADAR.

A NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF0 TORNADO ALONG MILL CREEK ROAD. MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS WERE 65 MPH. THE DURATION WAS 1 MINUTE, WITH A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 40 YARDS AND A PATH LENGTH OF 0.50 MILES. THE DAMAGE PATH STARTED JUST EAST OF WJ JONES LANE ALONG MILL CREEK ROAD AND CONTINUED NORTHEAST TOWARD THE INTERSECTION OF MILL CREEK ROAD AND DOWTY ROAD. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF BLOWN CRAB POTS AND MINOR LIMB DAMAGE.
